eBay First Stream · GMV Conversion Analysis
Jan 2026 – Jun 2026 · Data refresh: 2026-06-09 · Filter by country & category per tab
Success = GMV > $0 on first stream  |  Comparison = GMV = $0 on first stream
First Stream GMV Conversion

What separates sellers who make a sale on their very first live stream from those who don't? All metrics are anchored to the first stream date only — no downstream data.

Success Definition (this dashboard)

Unlike the companion analysis (re-stream timing), this view uses a same-day outcome:

  • Success — GMV > $0: Seller's total GMV on their first stream date exceeds zero
  • Not success — GMV = $0: Seller streamed but recorded no GMV on the first stream date

Seller must have event_duration_min > 0 and deleted_ind = 0. Metrics are summed across all events on the first stream day (a seller may run multiple events).

✓ Real data embedded (Q5 + Q6) — 954 rows across 8 countries and 92 categories. Use the Country & Category filters below to slice any tab independently.
Country
Category
✓ Embedded · 8 countries · 92 categories
Overall metric comparison — converters vs non-converters

Sorted by strength of separation (|delta %| descending). Medians used where Q3 provides them; averages used for likes and unique viewers (* marked). Positive delta = converters are higher.

Pillar Metric (first stream) GMV > $0 GMV = $0 Delta %
Re-stream Pattern — within 21 days of first stream

Run Q8 and share results to enable this section.

Metric definitions
Seller cohort
Sellers
Unique sellers who ran their first-ever live stream in the selected time period.
Conv %
% of first-time streamers who sold ≥ 1 item (GMV > $0) during that first stream.
First-stream performance (converter sellers)
Avg / Median GMV
Gross merchandise value per converter seller (USD). Median is less affected by high-value outliers.
Avg duration (min)
Average stream length in minutes.
Avg viewers
Average peak concurrent viewer count during the stream.
Avg unique viewers
Average count of distinct viewers across the full stream.
Avg chat
Average total chat messages received during the stream.
Avg likes
Average total likes received.
Items sold (avg)
Average number of items sold during the first stream; converter sellers only.
Retention
% Repeat Streamer
% of first-time streamers who went live at least once more after their first stream.
Re-stream pattern
When re-streamed
How quickly the seller returned to stream after their first event. 0–7 / 8–15 / 16–21 = returned within that many days of first stream. 21+ / never = did not re-stream within 21 days.
% sold on 1st stream
% of sellers in this bucket who had GMV > $0 on their very first stream — independent of whether they re-streamed.
Active sellers
Sellers who held at least one stream in that time window (days 1–7, 8–15, or 16–21 after first stream).
Streams
Total stream sessions held by this bucket's sellers in that window.
GMV
Total gross merchandise value generated in that window (USD).
% streams w/ sale
% of streams in this window where at least one item was sold.

Re-stream Pattern — within 21 days of first stream

Run Q8 and share results to enable this section.

Metric definitions
Groups
Converter (Conv)
Seller whose first stream generated GMV > $0 — sold at least one item.
Non-converter (Non-conv)
Seller whose first stream generated GMV = $0 — no items sold.
Columns
Avg
Arithmetic mean across all sellers in that group for the metric shown.
Median
Middle value; more robust than the mean for right-skewed distributions like GMV.
Delta
Median converter value minus median non-converter value, showing the gap in typical first-stream experience.
Metrics
GMV
Gross merchandise value generated during the first stream (USD). Non-converters are always $0.
Items sold
Number of items sold during the first stream. Non-converters are always 0.
Duration (min)
Length of the first stream session in minutes.
Viewers
Peak concurrent viewer count during the stream.
Unique viewers
Count of distinct viewers across the full stream.
Chat count
Total chat messages received during the stream.
Likes
Total likes received during the stream.
Retention
% Repeat Streamer
% of sellers who streamed again after their first session.
Re-stream pattern
When re-streamed
How quickly the seller returned to stream after their first event. 0–7 / 8–15 / 16–21 = returned within that many days of first stream. 21+ / never = did not re-stream within 21 days.
% sold on 1st stream
% of sellers in this bucket who had GMV > $0 on their very first stream — independent of whether they re-streamed.
Active sellers
Sellers who held at least one stream in that time window (days 1–7, 8–15, or 16–21 after first stream).
Streams
Total stream sessions held by this bucket's sellers in that window.
GMV
Total gross merchandise value generated in that window (USD).
% streams w/ sale
% of streams in this window where at least one item was sold.
Engagement Signal Buckets — Conversion Rate by Threshold

Metric definitions
Signal buckets
Signal
Engagement dimension used to group sellers: stream duration, viewer count, chat count, or like count — all measured on the first stream.
Bucket
Range of signal values. Each seller is placed in exactly one bucket per signal based on their first-stream measurement.
Sellers
Count of first-time streamers in this signal × bucket segment.
Conv %
% of sellers in this bucket who sold ≥ 1 item on their first stream.
Med GMV
Median gross merchandise value per converter in this bucket (USD).
% Repeat
% of sellers in this bucket who went live again after their first session.
Re-stream pattern
When re-streamed
How quickly the seller returned to stream after their first event. 0–7 / 8–15 / 16–21 = returned within that many days of first stream. 21+ / never = did not re-stream within 21 days.
% sold on 1st stream
% of sellers in this bucket who had GMV > $0 on their very first stream — independent of whether they re-streamed.
Active sellers
Sellers who held at least one stream in that time window (days 1–7, 8–15, or 16–21 after first stream).
Streams
Total stream sessions held by this bucket's sellers in that window.
GMV
Total gross merchandise value generated in that window (USD).
% streams w/ sale
% of streams in this window where at least one item was sold.

Actionable Recommendations — First Stream GMV Conversion

Derived directly from Query 4 signal bucket data (n=3,361 sellers). Each recommendation targets a specific bucket gap, names the owner team, and quantifies the conversion lift if the threshold is crossed.

ℹ All-market data only. Numbers on this tab are based on the full dataset (n=3,361 sellers, all countries, all categories) and do not reflect any country or category filter applied on other tabs. For country- or category-specific conversion rates, use the Signal Buckets tab with filters applied.
Where sellers are today
3,361
First-stream sellers
all cohorts
523
No GMV on first stream
15.6% miss rate
2,838
GMV > $0 on first stream
84.4% base rate
~310
Highest-leverage target
chat 1–20 group
Notable anomaly — chat count: Sellers with zero chat convert at 52.7%, but sellers with just 1–20 chats convert at only 32.6%. Hypothesis: the zero-chat group includes established sellers with pre-existing buyer intent (PN-driven purchases) who don't need chat to convert, while the 1–20 chat group are genuinely new streamers who tried but failed to build momentum. Recommendation: segment these two zero-chat sub-groups before treating them as a single cohort.
Lever 1 — Duration  ·  Highest addressability, fast win
1
Send a "keep going" nudge at the 25-minute mark
In-stream product · Seller app / streaming tool
5.3%
<10 min → GMV > $0
45.5%
10–30 min conversion
88.0%
61–120 min conversion
75 sellers
currently <10 min
  • At the 25-min mark, surface a banner in the streamer dashboard: "Sellers who stream 60+ min convert at 88% — you're 25 min in, keep going!"
  • At 55 min, send a second nudge: "Top 10% of sellers stream 2+ hours. Your viewers are still watching."
  • Show real-time conversion probability score as a live gauge (derived from duration + current viewer count).
  • Separate action for the 154 sellers in the 10–30 min group: post-stream email with a personalized replay showing their drop-off point vs. cohort median (121 min for converters).
Engineering Seller Experience  Expected lift if 10–30 min group reaches 60 min: +42 pp conversion on ~229 sellers ≈ ~96 additional GMV events
2
Pre-stream checklist with a "minimum viable stream" duration goal
Seller onboarding · Go-live coaching
229
sellers under 60 min
93.9%
120 min+ conversion rate
$3,674
avg GMV at 120 min+
  • Before the first stream, require sellers to complete a "Stream Plan" (topic, item list, duration target). Default target: 60 min minimum.
  • In the go-live confirmation screen, show the conversion rate ladder: "30 min → 68% · 60 min → 88% · 120 min → 94%" — make the data visible before they start.
  • Add a duration achievement badge visible to viewers ("2-hour streamer") to gamify longer sessions.
  • Inventory readiness gate: before the go-live confirmation screen, check that the seller has ≥ 40 active listings. Converter sellers sell a median of 42 items in their first stream — running out of stock mid-stream breaks momentum and prevents buyers from adding to cart. Surface a warning: "Top sellers list 50+ items before going live."
Seller Experience Content  Low engineering cost; high leverage on the new-seller drop-off pattern
Lever 2 — Chat engagement  ·  Biggest conversion gap, requires audience seeding
3
Seed opening questions via eBay Live moderation team for first-time sellers
Live Operations · Community team
32.6%
1–20 chats conversion
80.6%
21–100 chats conversion
99.7%
500+ chats conversion
310
sellers in 1–20 chat bucket
  • Assign a "stream success" moderator to every first-time seller's first 30 minutes. Their job: post 3–5 opener questions (item condition? what's your story? limited quantity?) to break the chat silence.
  • Getting from 1–20 to 21+ chats crosses the 80% conversion threshold — even a small mod intervention achieves this.
  • Build an automated "question bank" tailored by seller category (collectibles, fashion, electronics) so the mod doesn't need to improvise.
Live Ops Community  Expected lift: +48 pp on 310 sellers ≈ ~149 additional conversions per analysis period
4
Give sellers a "question bank" template before their first stream
Seller enablement · Pre-stream coaching email
205
sellers with zero chat
52.7%
their conversion rate
$1,301
avg GMV (zero-chat group)
  • Send a pre-stream email 48h before the first stream with 10 category-specific chat prompts: "Ask your viewers: Would you like a close-up of the stitching?"
  • Include the insight: "Sellers who get 21+ chat messages convert at 80%. Here's how to start the conversation."
  • The zero-chat group at 52.7% is actually doing OK — focus the email on the 310 sellers in 1–20 chat who are underperforming relative to both zero-chat and 21+ chat groups.
Marketing Seller Experience  Zero engineering cost; purely content and email automation
Lever 3 — Viewer acquisition  ·  Highest impact, requires platform distribution
5
Boost first-time seller streams on category browse pages at go-live
Marketplace · Discovery algorithm · Engineering
5.8%
1–10 viewers conversion
47.6%
11–50 viewers conversion
80.6%
51–200 viewers conversion
95.0%
200+ viewers conversion
  • Getting a new seller from 1–10 viewers to 51–200 viewers jumps conversion from 5.8% → 80.6%. The viewer threshold is the single biggest unlock.
  • Automatically surface first-time seller streams in the top 3 slots of the relevant category browse page for the first 60 minutes — label them "New Seller Live" to set expectations and drive curiosity clicks.
  • Trigger a push notification to buyers who have purchased from similar sellers in the last 90 days: "A new seller in [category] is live right now."
  • eBay followers of the seller should receive a push at stream start — verify this is actually firing for all new sellers (check notification opt-in rates).
Engineering Marketplace  High complexity; highest potential lift — 69 sellers currently stuck at 1–10 viewers with 5.8% conversion
6
Make "like" prompts more prominent — low-friction signal with strong conversion correlation
In-stream UX · Viewer-side product
54.8%
zero-likes conversion
83.1%
51–200 likes conversion
96.1%
1,000+ likes conversion
$4,504
avg GMV at 1,000+ likes
  • Like count is monotonically predictive (54.8% → 96.1%) and is a viewer-side action — sellers can't force it, but eBay can surface it more prominently.
  • During new-seller streams, show a floating "❤ Like this stream" prompt to viewers every 10 minutes if they haven't liked yet.
  • Show sellers a real-time like count in their dashboard with a goal marker: "50 likes = 83% conversion probability."
  • Test: seller verbally asking for likes ("tap the heart if you like what you're seeing") — build this into the pre-stream coaching script.
Engineering Marketing  394 sellers currently have zero likes at 54.8% — a reachable group with a 28 pp upside to 51–200 like tier
Lever 4 — Post-stream recovery  ·  Capture the 15.6% who didn't convert
7
Personalized post-stream debrief for non-converters within 2 hours of stream end
Seller CRM · Email / Push · Data pipeline
523
non-converting first streams
66–87%
non-converters who re-stream
<2h
ideal debrief window
  • Non-converters still re-stream at 66–87% (By Week data) — they are not churning. The intervention is about stream 2, not stream 1 retention.
  • Within 2 hours of stream end, send a personalized email: "Here's what happened in your first stream and what to try next time" — include their duration vs. the 121-min converter median, their viewer count vs. the 270 converter median, and their chat count vs. the 158 converter median.
  • For sellers whose gap is duration: "Your stream was X min shorter than the average seller who made a sale. Consider scheduling a 2-hour block next time."
  • For sellers whose gap is viewers: "Your viewers were below the 50-viewer threshold where conversion jumps to 80%. Schedule your next stream and we'll promote it to [N] buyers in [category]."
  • Include a one-click "Schedule Stream 2" button in the email — remove friction from the re-attempt decision.
Engineering CRM Live Ops  Addresses 523 sellers who missed first-stream conversion; re-stream rate suggests high receptivity
8
Investigate and segment the zero-chat 52.7% converter group
Analytics · Strategy · Seller segmentation
205
zero-chat sellers
52.7%
their conversion rate
$1,301
avg GMV — zero-chat group
  • The zero-chat group outperforms the 1–20 chat group (52.7% vs 32.6%). This is the anomaly flagged above — these two groups likely represent different seller archetypes.
  • Hypothesis A: zero-chat converters are existing eBay powersellers with pre-built buyer audiences who buy via push notification, not live engagement.
  • Hypothesis B: zero-chat converters had very short, focused "buy this now" streams with pre-arranged buyers — not traditional live commerce.
  • Action: pull slr_id for the 205 sellers, cross-reference with GMV history before first stream, and segment into "existing sellers going live" vs "net new sellers." Treatment should differ entirely for each group.
Analytics Strategy  This segment analysis should be completed before deploying chat interventions that might not apply here
Lever 5 — Re-stream velocity  ·  The 7-day habit window is the highest-value unlock
9
Show a "Book Stream 2" scheduling flow the moment Stream 1 ends
In-app product · Seller app · Post-stream flow
72%
re-streamers return within 7 days
$34,215
avg 21-day GMV per 0–7 day seller
18%
never return within 21 days
570
sellers generating $0 follow-on GMV
  • Within 5 minutes of a first stream ending, surface a full-screen modal: "Great first stream! Sellers who go live again within 7 days generate 30× more GMV in their first month. Pick a date this week."
  • Pre-populate a suggested time slot: same time of day, 7 days from now. One tap to confirm, no calendar app required.
  • If the seller dismisses, re-surface the scheduling prompt in the seller hub homepage for the next 48 hours.
  • For converters: frame it as momentum — "You made your first sales. Your audience is warm right now — don't let 7 days pass." For non-converters: frame it as a retry — "Most sellers find their rhythm on Stream 2. Here's what to try next time."
Engineering Seller Experience  Targets all 3,361 first-time streamers. Even converting 20% of the 570 never-returners = ~114 sellers × $34K each = ~$3.9M incremental 21-day GMV
10
Day 5 urgency push — re-engage at-risk sellers before the 7-day window closes
Seller CRM · Push notification · Email
Day 7
7-day window closes
8%
return in days 8–15 (vs 72% in 0–7)
81%
of 0–7 day sellers still active in week 2
71%
still active in week 3
  • At Day 5 post-first-stream, if Stream 2 is not yet scheduled, trigger a push + email: "You have 2 days left in your peak window — 72% of live sellers stream again within a week. Your audience still remembers you. [Schedule now →]"
  • Include the retention data as social proof: "Sellers who stream within 7 days stay 81% active through the following week. Don't let the momentum break."
  • At Day 7 (final warning), if still unscheduled, send a last-chance SMS (for opted-in sellers): "Today is your peak re-stream day. Go live for even 30 minutes — your buyers are still watching."
  • A/B test framing: loss aversion ("Don't lose your audience") vs. positive reinforcement ("Your viewers are ready for more") — data suggests both work but loss framing may be stronger for new sellers.
CRM Engineering  Target: ~600 sellers who haven't booked by Day 5. Getting 30% back within 7 days ≈ 180 sellers × $34K = $6.1M incremental GMV pipeline
Prioritization at a glance
#ActionTarget sellers Conversion lift (pp)EffortOwner
1In-stream duration nudge at 25 min229+42 ppLowEng
2Pre-stream duration goal in go-live checklistAll new+20–42 ppLowContent
3Mod team seeds chat for first 30 min310+48 ppMediumOps
4Pre-stream "question bank" emailAll new+15–48 ppLowCRM

Actions 1, 2, 4, 6, 10 can be initiated this sprint with minimal engineering. Action 3 (mod seeding) and Action 9 (post-stream booking modal) can begin as a manual pilot next week. Action 5 (category boost) is the highest-leverage discovery change — prioritize for next quarter. Action 9 + 10 together form the re-stream habit loop and should ship as a paired feature.

Methodology — First Stream GMV Conversion

This dashboard answers a single question: what attributes of a seller's first live stream predict whether they will make a sale that day? The companion dashboard (First-Stream-Analysis_20260615.html) uses re-stream timing as the success metric; this one uses same-day GMV outcome.

Success definition

GroupConditionField
SuccessGMV > $0 on first stream datemade_sale_flag = 1
Not successGMV = $0 on first stream datemade_sale_flag = 0
First streamEarliest cal_dt with event_duration_min > 0 and deleted_ind = 0ROW_NUMBER() rank = 1
Cohort start2026-01-01 onwardscal_dt >= '2026-01-01'

Source tables

TableUsed for
P_AMER_VERTICALS_T.LIVE_COMMERCE_DAILY_DEMAND_INDICATORSGMV, sold items, viewers, buyers, bidders, duration, country (slr_cntry_cd), category (l1_categ_nm)
P_AMER_VERTICALS_T.LIVE_COMMERCE_DAILY_DEMAND_INDICATORS_DEDUPEChat count, like count, unique viewer count

Query 3 / Query 5 — Converter vs Non-Converter by Cohort Week

Groups sellers by DATE_TRUNC('week', first_stream_date) and computes avg + median for every engagement metric, separately for made_sale_flag = 1 and made_sale_flag = 0. Query 5 is Query 3 extended with slr_cntry_cd and l1_categ_nm GROUP BY columns. Country and category breakdowns are embedded — use filter bars to slice directly.

Query 4 / Query 6 — Signal Bucket Analysis

Divides sellers into buckets by each engagement signal and computes pct_made_sale per bucket. Query 6 is Query 4 extended with country and category dimensions. Signal bucket data is embedded with country and category dimensions.

SignalBuckets
viewer_count1–10  |  11–50  |  51–200  |  200+
stream_duration_min<10 min  |  10–30 min  |  31–60 min  |  61–120 min  |  120 min+
chat_count0  |  1–20  |  21–100  |  101–500  |  500+
like_count0  |  1–50  |  51–200  |  201–1,000  |  1,000+

Query 7 — Items Sold on First Stream

Extends Q5 by adding si_during (sold-item count) from LIVE_COMMERCE_DAILY_DEMAND_INDICATORS. Two new output columns: cv_avg_items_sold and cv_med_items_sold (converter side only — non-converters by definition sold 0 items). Once Q7 CSV is embedded, the Overview and Metric Comparison tabs gain an "Items sold" row.

Query 8 — Re-stream Timing Buckets (21-day pattern)

For every seller who had a first stream in 2026, identifies when (if ever) they streamed again within 21 days. Sellers are bucketed by days-to-first-restream:

BucketMeaning
0–7 daysRe-streamed within the first week
8–15 daysRe-streamed in days 8–15
16–21 daysRe-streamed in days 16–21
21+ / neverDid not re-stream within 21 days (includes late re-streamers and those who never returned)

For each bucket, the 21-day window is divided into three sub-windows (days 1–7, 8–15, 16–21) showing active sellers, stream count, total GMV, and % streams with a sale. Output: country × category × restream_bucket. The Re-stream Pattern section appears at the bottom of all four filter tabs, filtered by each tab's country/category selection.

How country & category filters work

  1. Data is already embedded — Q5 (954 rows, 8 countries, 92 categories) and Q6 (1,393 rows) are baked into this file. No upload required.
  2. Select a country — the category dropdown automatically narrows to categories available in that country. When "All" countries is selected, the category dropdown shows the top 12 categories by seller volume across all markets.
  3. Filters are per-tab and independent — Overview, Metric Comparison, Signal Buckets, and By Week each have their own Country + Category selection.
  4. Weighted averages are used when re-aggregating across multiple cohort weeks after filtering. Medians shown are weighted averages of per-group medians (approximation).
  5. % Repeat Streamer (By Week and Signal Buckets tabs) is only available in the unfiltered All / All view — this field comes from Query 3 / Query 4 and is not in the Q5 / Q6 segmented data. Filtered views show "—" for that column.
  6. Actions tab is always all-market (n=3,361). Its numbers do not change with filter selections — see Signal Buckets with a filter applied for country- or category-specific conversion rates.

Primary questionDoes first-stream performance predict survival?What drives a sale on day 1?
